items.titel: ​The EBA launches Discussion Paper on Pillar 3 Data Hub for small banks
items.samenvatting: ​The European Banking Authority (EBA) today published a Discussion Paper which proposes a simplified process for small and non-complex institutions (SNCIs) when implementing the Pillar 3 Data Hub (P3DH). The objective is to gather stakeholder feedback on a streamlined approach under which the EBA wo

items.titel: ESAs publish the first report on DORA major ICT-related incidents
items.samenvatting: The European Supervisory Authorities (EBA, EIOPA and ESMA) today published their first annual overview of major ICT-related incidents in the EU financial sector based on a reporting mechanism established by the Digital Operational Resilience Act (DORA). It shows that ICT risks are increasingly borde

items.titel: ​The EBA and the New York State Department of Financial Services sign a Memorandum of Understanding to foster cooperation in the supervision of international stablecoin activities
items.samenvatting: ​The European Banking Authority (EBA) has signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with the New York State Department of Financial Services (NYDFS) under the Markets in Crypto-Assets Regulation (MiCA). The agreement aims to strengthen cooperation in the supervision of entities engaged in cross-bor

items.titel: ​The EBA updates list of other systemically important institutions
items.samenvatting: ​The European Banking Authority (EBA) today updated the list of other systemically important institutions (O-SIIs) in the EU, based on notifications received from national authorities during 2025. The publication aims to increase transparency and provides a consistent overview of institutions identi

items.titel: Bafin's Organisation chart
items.samenvatting: Organisation chart of the Federal Financial Supervisory Authority

items.titel: Guidance: UK Financial Sanctions FAQs
items.samenvatting: OFSI publishes FAQs providing short-form guidance and technical information on financial sanctions.

items.titel: European Prosecutors Seize Assets in Italian Subsidy Fraud Probe
Egyptian Tycoon Detained in Organized Crime Probe
A $57 Billion Toll: How Evolving Scams Are Fleecing Europe
items.samenvatting: A new report reveals that criminal networks are rapidly adapting to new technologies, including artificial intelligence, siphoning billions from victims while the vast majority of crimes go unreported.

items.titel: Hungarian Prosecutors Vow Action on Graft Claims, but Target Anti-Corruption Chief
items.samenvatting: The national anti-corruption watchdog alleges billions were misappropriated during the Orbán era, but prosecutors revealed to OCCRP that the watchdog’s president is himself facing criminal charges.

items.titel: U.K. Targets $68M in Properties Owned by China-Born Man with Multiple Citizenships
items.samenvatting: Britain's land registry shows requests for freezing order on luxury properties owned by Yuan Yihua, who holds so-called “golden passports” issued in return for investments in Cyprus, Cambodia and Vanuatu.

items.titel: Eyes On The Crew: The Russian Watchmen Aboard Moscow's Sanctioned ‘Shadow Fleet’ Tankers
items.samenvatting: Dozens of men with security backgrounds have been quietly sailing alongside the standard crews on the tankers carrying sanctioned Russian oil. Now, reporters speak to some of these "watchmen" firsthand to learn about their roles on board.

items.titel: EU Sanctions Iranian Officials and Revolutionary Guard Unit Over Maritime Disruptions
Amnesty Report: Cambodia’s Crackdown on Scam Compounds Failed to Protect Victims
Prosecutors Seek Up to Two Years in Prison for Russian Woman Convicted of Lying About Spy Ties
items.samenvatting: Federal prosecutors are urging a judge to make an example of Nomma Zarubina, who was jailed pre-trial after allegedly stalking and harassing the FBI agent investigating her.

items.titel: Dutch Cocaine Kingpin Sheltering in Sierra Leone Allegedly Organized Shipment Seized in Record Bust
items.samenvatting: Fueling frustration for Sierra Leone parliamentarians, fugitive Dutch trafficker Joseph Leijdekkers allegedly masterminded a 30-ton cocaine shipment from his West African hideout, Spanish authorities told OCCRP. Leijdekkers has not been charged in the case.

items.titel: As the 2026 World Cup Looms, a Shadow Tournament of Cyber Fraud Begins
items.samenvatting: Just days before the opening match, cybersecurity experts and the F.B.I. are warning fans about a sophisticated wave of fake websites, malicious betting apps, and data-stealing traps.

items.titel: Indonesian Deputy Minister Detained in Immigration Extortion Probe
Thailand’s Ex-PM Thaksin Freed Early After Royal Pardon
“Predatorgate” Wins Top Award at Second Floodlight Investigative Journalism Game Jam
items.samenvatting: The winning game prototype, described as a “love letter to old school journalism,” is based on an investigative report into Predator spyware by Athens-based outlet Inside Story.

items.titel: Millions in Cash, Gold, and Assault Rifles Seized in Arrest of Iraqi Oil Official
items.samenvatting: The detention of a deputy oil minister marks a dramatic escalation in Iraq’s new anti-corruption purge, signaling a crackdown on graft within the country’s most vital economic sector.

items.titel: Armenia Asked to Freeze Magnitsky-Linked Funds
items.samenvatting: After money tied to a notorious Russian tax fraud slipped into Armenia's financial system unnoticed by regulators, the victim is stepping in to demand an immediate asset freeze.

items.titel: Dozens of Azerbaijan Companies Manage Russian ‘Shadow Fleet’ Tankers
items.samenvatting: OCCRP has obtained corporate documents for 31 companies registered in Azerbaijan, which have managed the safety aspects of “shadow fleet” tankers that avoid sanctions in order to trade in Russian oil and other petroleum products.

items.titel: Ecuadorian Cartel Boss Fights Extradition in Spain, Accusing President Noboa of Assassination Plot
items.samenvatting: Wilmer Chavarría, the presumed leader of the Los Lobos syndicate, claims the Ecuadorian government wants him silenced because he claims the president is tied to drug trafficking and political murder.

items.titel: Albania Freezes Assets in Kushner Resort Probe
items.samenvatting: A widening property-fraud investigation into a $4 billion luxury resort linked to Jared Kushner has led Albanian authorities to freeze developer assets, compounding pressure from public protests and international critics.

items.titel: Romanian Army Chief Investigated in University Admissions Scandal
US Requests Australia Extradite Jet Ski Salesman Who Created Hundreds of Shell Firms
items.samenvatting: Australian authorities confirmed the American extradition request for Ian Taylor on money laundering charges. OCCRP previously reported that the Taylor family set up offshore firms that were used by crime groups.

items.titel: Warnings of Human Trafficking Issued for 2026 FIFA World Cup
items.samenvatting: With millions expected to descend on North America for the tournament, officials say criminal networks are using fraudulent employment offers to lure victims into sexual exploitation and forced labor.

items.titel: Deutschland will Kampfjet ohne Frankreich bauen
items.samenvatting: Für rund 500 Milliarden Euro sollten Airbus und Dassault einen Gegenentwurf zum US-Kampfjet F35 entwickeln. Deutschland will sich nun neue Partner suchen.

items.titel: Kein Ukraine-Bezug: Foto von geretteter Katze ist aus den USA
items.samenvatting: Online sollen Zweifel an der Berichterstattung zur Ukraine gesät werden: Das Foto von einer Frau, die ihre Katze nach einem Tornado in den USA rettete, sei für Berichte über einen Bombenangriff in der Ukraine verwendet worden. Doch das stimmt nicht.

items.titel: Das überrollte System
items.samenvatting: Die Pillen werden über Instagram oder TikTok bestellt: Suchtkliniken schlagen Alarm wegen einer wachsenden Zahl von Opiodabhängigen. Wie groß ist das Problem wirklich? CORRECTIV und DER SPIEGEL starten eine Umfrage unter Betroffenen und Angehörigen.

items.titel: Fraktionsübergreifend: EU-Abgeordnete wollen sich für Verbot der AfD-Europapartei ESN einsetzen
items.samenvatting: Die EU-Aufsichtsbehörde sieht Hinweise auf schwere Verstöße gegen EU-Grundwerte bei allen neun Mitgliedsparteien der ESN – darunter die AfD. Mehrere Fraktionen wollen das Verfahren jetzt anstoßen.

items.titel: Organspenderegister: Was passiert, wenn die Widerspruchslösung kommt?
items.samenvatting: Seit zwei Jahren können Menschen in einem zentralen Organspenderegister ihren Haltung zur Organspende hinterlegen. Bislang haben dies rund 500.000 Menschen getan – viel zu wenige, um den Bedarf an Spenderorganen zu decken. Jetzt wollen Bundestagsabgeordnete einen neuen Anlauf bei der sogenannten Wid

items.titel: Pfizer-Dokument belegt keine 80 Prozent Fehlgeburten bei geimpften Schwangeren
items.samenvatting: Online heißt es, 80 Prozent der Schwangeren hätten während der Teilnahme an einer Covid-19-Impfstudie von Pfizer ihre Babys verloren. Die Behauptung wird in ähnlicher Form auch von der US-Impfgegnerin Naomi Wolf verbreitet. Doch sie ist falsch.

items.titel: CORRECTIV entwickelt Spiel: „Projekt G“ macht Journalismus erlebbar
items.samenvatting: Für Spieleabende, Workshops und Bildungsprogramme: Mit dem Spiel „Projekt G“ macht CORRECTIV investigative Recherchen erlebbar. Die Spielenden decken ein geheimes Treffen mit bedrohlichen Plänen auf und müssen ihre Ergebnisse vor der Chefredaktion verteidigen.

items.titel: AfD-Europapartei ESN droht Verlust von Status und Millionen-Förderung
items.samenvatting: Ein Bericht der EU-Aufsichtsbehörde sieht Hinweise auf Verstöße der europäischen Rechtsaußenpartei ESN gegen EU-Grundwerte. Das könnte drastische Folgen für die Parteienfamilie der AfD nach sich ziehen. CORRECTIV liegen die Belege vor.

items.titel: Wo reicht Ihr Geld zum Wohnen?
items.samenvatting: Wo können Sie sich mit Ihrem Einkommen Wohnen noch leisten – und wo nicht? Und wie viel Prozent Ihres Nettoeinkommens gehen fürs Wohnen drauf? Die Antworten verrät Ihnen unser Wohnometer, das auf exklusiv von CORRECTIV.Schweiz ausgewerteten Miet- und Kaufpreisen basiert.

items.titel: Wo Russlands Propaganda an der Realität zerschellt
items.samenvatting: Russlands Propaganda-Maschinerie läuft. Eines der Zielländer ist Armenien, wo am 7. Juni eine Parlamentswahl stattfindet. Doch die alten Methoden verlieren ihre Wirkung in einem Land, das sich längst weiterentwickelt.

items.titel: Ein Leak enthüllt, wie Kreml, Geheimdienst und private Firmen zusammenarbeiten
items.samenvatting: Plastikskelette vor dem Brandenburger Tor und KI-Propaganda gegen Armeniens Premier: Ein Leak zeigt, wie Russland Desinformation und Sabotage koordiniert.

items.titel: CORRECTIV als bevorzugte Quelle bei Google einstellen
items.samenvatting: Möchten Sie die Recherchen, Nachrichten und Faktenchecks von CORRECTIV in der Google-Suche leichter finden? Mit einer neuen Funktion können Sie CORRECTIV als bevorzugte Quelle auswählen.

items.titel: Wegen psychischer Krankheit polizeibekannt?
items.samenvatting: In Hessen gilt seit einigen Monaten eine Meldepflicht für Menschen mit schweren psychischen Krankheiten – wenn Ärzte sie als potenziell gefährlich einstufen. Niedersachsen plant nun Ähnliches. Ein bedrohlicher Grundrechtseingriff – oder wirksamer Schutz für die Bevölkerung vor Gewalttaten?

items.titel: In Sekunden bestellt und geliefert: Ein Mietvertrag für die AfD
items.samenvatting: Flink präsentiert sich als diverses und weltoffenes Unternehmen. Gleichzeitig profitiert eine Firma der AfD von einem Mietvertrag mit dem Lieferdienst.

items.titel: Hat Pistorius beim Wehrdienstgesetz Rechtsbruch begangen?
items.samenvatting: Das Verteidigungsministerium setzt eine umstrittene Wehrdienstregel per Verfügung außer Kraft. Rechtsexperten werfen Minister Boris Pistorius vor, damit die Gewaltenteilung missachtet zu haben. CORRECTIV zeichnet die Debatte nach.

items.titel: „Remigrations“-Gipfel: AfD sucht Nähe zu Sellner und US-Hardlinern
items.samenvatting: Beim „Remigration Summit“ in Portugal vernetzt sich die rechtsextreme Szene in Europa rund um den Österreicher Martin Sellner. Auch die AfD ist mit Bundesvorstand Kay Gottschalk hochrangig vertreten – er holt sich Tipps beim früheren US-amerikanischen ICE-Befehlshaber Gregory Bovino.

items.titel: Brennholz gleich Klimaschutz? Was an dieser Aussage von Hubert Aiwanger dran ist
items.samenvatting: Brennholz sei gleich Klimaschutz, hat Bayerns Wirtschaftsminister Hubert Aiwanger online behauptet. Wie er darauf kommt und ob das stimmt, fragen einige in den Kommentaren. Wir haben bei Fachleuten nachgehakt – es zeigt sich: Die CO2-Bilanz von Holz ist facettenreich.

items.titel: Trump intelligence adviser previously helped father pursue millions from Kremlin-linked bank, leaked documents show
items.samenvatting: Amaryllis Fox Kennedy, who recently stepped down from two senior administration posts, was serving as a CIA officer during the dispute.

items.titel: Chinese spies are posing as recruiters to target officials and journalists
items.samenvatting: The U.S. and its Five Eyes partners warned that Chinese military intelligence services were behind “cover companies” using job platforms to pry sensitive information from foreigners.

items.titel: Mexico seizes suspicious Keytruda in raid to dismantle counterfeit medication ring
items.samenvatting: After ICIJ’s Cancer Calculus investigation exposed a market for falsified Keytruda, two sources confirmed Friday that Mexican authorities found vials labeled as the anti-cancer drug in the raid.

items.titel: Fidelity opened account for Epstein, even as outrage grew
items.samenvatting: Asset manager Fidelity moved millions for company owned by disgraced financier Jeffrey Epstein just weeks before his 2019 arrest, documents reveal.

items.titel: Tracing Digital Links Between Viory and Ruptly
items.samenvatting: “In the age of misinformation, the line between fact and fiction is blurrier than ever.” “For those of us working in video news, verification isn’t a nice-to-have. It’s a necessity. It is how we protect the stories we help shape and how we earn and maintain trust in an increasingly chaotic informati

items.titel: Europe Means Business on Cloud and AI Sovereignty
items.samenvatting: For Europe, technology dependence is a strategic liability. The European Commission’s Tech Sovereignty Package offers solutions, but can it hold its resolve?

items.titel: The Energy Supply Cliff is Alarmingly Near
items.samenvatting: With the Strait of Hormuz closed, national oil reserves have been steadily depleting, and will empty by the end of the summer, halting most economic activity, globally.

items.titel: No-Rules Based Order: The World As It Really Is
items.samenvatting: A veteran Singaporean diplomat and scholar reflects on today’s strategic uncertainties and enduring myths, appealing for calmer and more sober perspectives on the challenges.

items.titel: The Nathan Gill Case: Isolated Foreign Malign Interference Case or a Broader Hybrid Threat?
items.samenvatting: The Nathan Gill case exposes the blurred line between corruption and foreign malign interference. Strategic resilience requires seeing the adversary’s wider campaign.

items.titel: Armenia’s Election and the Future of Security in the South Caucasus
items.samenvatting: Testing Armenia’s Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan, future integration with Europe is a live issue in the country’s first election since military defeat in Nagorno-Karabakh.

items.titel: The DPRK’s Chemical Facilities: Sunchon Area: Site Profile 7
items.samenvatting: This report on Sunchon chemical facilities, co-published by RUSI, VERTIC and 38 North, is the seventh Project Anthracite site profile exploring different chemical production facilities throughout North Korea.

items.titel: Reforming Defence: Lessons from the UK Defence Restructuring History
items.samenvatting: Post-Cold War defence reviews have all promised institutional transformation; most underdelivered. Understanding why requires an honest appraisal of past lessons.

items.titel: The DPRK’s Chemical Facilities: 8th February Vinalon Complex: Site Profile 8
items.samenvatting: This report on the 8th February Vinalon Complex, co-published by RUSI, VERTIC and 38 North, is the eighth and final Project Anthracite site profile exploring different chemical production facilities throughout North Korea.

items.titel: Power Under Dependency: Vulnerabilities in France’s Strategic Posture
items.samenvatting: Beyond holding assets, France’s resilience depends on establishing credible access, prioritisation, and substitution mechanisms for critical materials.

items.titel: Re-Establishing Japan’s Intelligence Capability – ‘Spy Paradise’ lost?
items.samenvatting: Japan has had enough of being a ‘spy paradise’: lacking key elements of intelligence capability and enduring society-wide vulnerability to foreign espionage and influence.

items.titel: Episode 121: No Easy Off-Ramp: Iran, the US and the Search for a Deal
items.samenvatting: Are we seeing the potential for a diplomatic off-ramp in the latest phase of the Iran crisis, or just another pause in a much longer confrontation?

items.titel: Illegal High Street Enterprise. Closed for Business, Open for Crime
items.samenvatting: Crime is running rife on Britain's high streets. Could the EU’s ‘administrative approach’ support better enforcement?

items.titel: Hollowing Out Lebanon: How Pressure on Hezbollah Could Save It
items.samenvatting: Israel’s campaign to disarm of Hezbollah may delegitimise the Lebanese state faster than it degrades the militia, reproducing the conditions that have so far sustained Hezbollah.

items.titel: Who Rules Cyberspace? The Microsoft Approach to Cyber Diplomacy
items.samenvatting: In the final episode of our Cyber Statecraft series, we invite Microsoft – a company that operates across nearly every jurisdiction on Earth – to the conversation.

items.titel: Episode 19: Adversarial Strategy: Russia’s Preparations for a Long War
items.samenvatting: We look at how Russia’s armed forces are learning lessons from combat and examine Moscow’s ambitions in its war against Ukraine.

items.titel: The Inextricable Link Between Geopolitics, Security and Humanitarian Impact
items.samenvatting: As big as the change at the end of the Cold War, the current 'hinge moment' is different in its complexities, discusses former UK Foreign Secretary David Miliband.

items.titel: Duke of Wellington Medal for Military History 2025
items.samenvatting: We are pleased to announce the winner for the 2025 Duke of Wellington Medal for Military History.

items.titel: Crypto regulatory affairs: New York and European supervisors to collaborate
items.samenvatting: In this first June edition of crypto regulatory affairs, we will cover:

items.titel: What banks need to know about GENIUS Act stablecoin compliance
items.samenvatting: Under the GENIUS Act, every stablecoin your bank touches will be either permitted under federal law or not. Treating a non-permitted stablecoin as if it were permitted will be a compliance failure, but most existing compliance systems are not built to make the distinction yet.

items.titel: OFAC sanctions Nobitex and three other Iranian cryptoasset exchanges
items.samenvatting: OFAC has designated Nobitex, Wallex, Bitpin and Ramzinex, plus Nobitex's chairman, two co-founders and its current CEO. Elliptic analysis shows that these exchanges have sent or received at least $40 billion in cryptoassets. Iranian cryptoasset exchanges were already blocked under existing sanctions

items.titel: Crypto governance starts with the model that already works
items.samenvatting: The cryptoasset industry has spent considerable energy arguing that cryptoassets are different enough to require a fundamentally new approach to governance.
